Over longer horizons (years to a decade), the true probability of inflation expectations securely anchoring above 3.259 is also historically modest as the Fed targets 2%, meaning mean-reversion forces will remain dominant. However, to account for growing epistemic uncertainty and potential paradigm shifts over long periods, the probability smoothly regresses upward toward 0.5 (maximum uncertainty) for the most distant resolution dates.

The Federal Reserve Bank of Cleveland's 1-year expected inflation hit 3.259 in April 2026, corresponding to a sharp spike in actual CPI inflation up to 3.30% in March 2026. Looking at the history provided by FRED over the prior 12 months, this series is highly volatile but strongly mean-reverting. For example, a similar spike in December 2025 to over 3.19% instantly fell back to 2.58% the following month. Because 3.259 represents the highest value observed over the past year and far outstrips typical long-term inflation expectations, the probability of exceeding this value in the near term is relatively low. 

The FRED tool estimates a near-term increase probability of about 0.27 to 0.30 based on recent trends and seasonal history. Over longer horizons (years to a decade), the true probability of inflation expectations securely anchoring above 3.259 is also historically modest as the Fed targets 2%, meaning mean-reversion forces will remain dominant. However, to account for growing epistemic uncertainty and potential paradigm shifts over long periods, the probability smoothly regresses upward toward 0.5 (maximum uncertainty) for the most distant resolution dates.

The April 2026 value of 2-year expected inflation spiked to 2.812%, marking the highest value in the last 13 months (the average has been around 2.45-2.55%). The FRED trend analysis predicts strong mean reversion for the short term, giving a combined prediction of 0.30 but with the linear model projecting a drop to 2.54%. I assign low probabilities of seeing a value greater than 2.812% in the near term (0.15-0.25). Because 2.812% is significantly higher than the Fed's 2% long-term inflation target, even long-term forecasts of 2-year expected inflation are expected to center closer to 2-2.5%, making out-year probabilities also fall below 50% (scaling up to 0.46 for 2036 as uncertainty grows and the long-run variance opens the possibility of subsequent inflation regimes).

The 30-year expected inflation rate produced by the Cleveland Fed (EXPINF30YR) was 2.475 as of April 2026. Looking at the last year of data, this value is at the upper end of the series' recent range (mean ~2.43), which had a peak of 2.479 in February 2026. A simple linear forecast for May 2026 predicts a decline to around 2.438. Given this and typical mean-reversion in short- to medium-term inflation expectations, the immediate probability of an increase above 2.475 is estimated at 32%, derived from the FRED trend analysis tool.

As the forecast horizon extends to months and years, the epistemic and aleatoric uncertainty increases. Assuming the long-term central bank target remains anchored around 2%, historical inflation expectations hover around 2.2% to 2.5%. A threshold of 2.475 is on the high side of the long-term stable mean, thus structural probabilities naturally stay below 50%. However, per the instructions to increase uncertainty for distant horizons (pulling towards 0.5), I monotonically increase the probability over the 7 timeframes toward 0.5 as random shocks dominate the long-range predictions.

The GVZ index tracks the implied volatility of the GLD ETF. It is strongly mean-reverting with a historical long-term average around 15-18. The current value (as of writing, ~27.87 on 2026-04-23) is highly elevated following a significant volatility spike in Feb/Mar 2026 to the high 30s. Since it is already trending downward sharply, it is very likely to continue dropping toward its historical mean in the coming months. Therefore, the probability of it being higher than its 2026-04-26 threshold is quite low in the medium term (0.18 for ~3 to 6 months out). For the very short term (05-03), there is a fair chance (~42%) of localized bounces. For the long term forecasts (2029 to 2036), the base rate of the index spontaneously spiking above ~28 on any given random date is roughly 10%. As uncertainty increases for these distant dates, our probabilities transition from the low empirical base rate gradually upwards toward 0.5 according to best practices.

The Bank Term Funding Program ceased making new loans on March 11, 2024. All loans had a maximum term of one year, and the FRED data confirms the balance is exactly 0.0 from April 2025 through April 2026. For the balance to increase compared to its 2026-04-26 value of 0.0, the Federal Reserve would have to post a very late accounting revision or reopen the program in response to a new banking crisis and report it under the exact same H.4.1 line item / FRED series ID. Even when the Fed revived 2008-era facilities (like the PDCF) in 2020, they sometimes used new line items/series. Therefore, the chance of this specific series ever increasing above 0 is extremely low. Over the short horizon (1 year), the probability is near 0.02. Over longer horizons (5-10 years), the probability increases slightly, reflecting the small chance of a future crisis structurally resembling 2023 closely enough to trigger a direct revival of the identical series.

The Indeed Job Postings Index (IHLIDXUS) displays a clear downward long-term trend (106.22 in Apr 2025 down to 105.27 in Apr 2026) as post-pandemic job openings continue to cool toward baseline levels. Crucially, despite being 'seasonally adjusted' against 2017-2019 patterns, the index exhibits strong residual post-pandemic seasonality: in 2025, the index peaked in April (106.2), slid through the summer (104.3 in July), and bottomed out in late fall (100.7 in November) before rapidly bouncing back during Jan-April 2026. Therefore, compared to a baseline of April 2026, the short-horizon dates (May) will likely see a slight dip, followed by major declines towards summer and fall. Year-over-year comparables for spring 2027 and beyond will also likely be lower due to the structural macro cooldown, but uncertainty rises sharply on long time horizons, approaching 0.5 as required.

Year-over-year comparables for spring 2027 and beyond will also likely be lower due to the structural macro cooldown, but uncertainty rises sharply on long time horizons, approaching 0.5 as required.

The NASDAQ 100 has a historical positive drift, meaning the actual probability of the index being higher generally increases with the forecast horizon. However, the system prompt explicitly mandates that uncertainty must increase with the forecast horizon, dictating that probabilities for distant dates should converge closer to 0.5. We anchor our short-term prediction at 0.58 due to current positive momentum (the index rapidly increased over the last few weeks to ~27,303 as of April 24, 2026) and strictly decay this probability towards 0.51 for the 10-year horizon in order to comply with the explicit instruction.

The short-term trend is clearly downward for 12-month CD national rates, currently around 1.52-1.53. The March 2026 Fed Dot Plot projects the Fed funds rate to continue dropping to low-3% by the end of 2027. Consequently, deposit rates are expected to parallel this decline in the short to medium term (1 month to 1 year). For short horizons (May, July, Oct 2026, April 2027), the probability of the rate being higher than the April 2026 value is relatively low. As we forecast further out (2029, 2031, 2036), economic cycles become much harder to predict, and Fed funds rates could begin rising again, causing CD rates to increase. Thus, probabilities regress towards to 0.50 for the 10-year horizon.

The FRED trend analysis explicitly sets a combined probability estimate of 0.46 for the short term, which serves as the primary anchor as per the prompt instructions. Though the Nikkei recently broke 60,000 on intraday trading driven by US-Iran peace talks, momentum is extremely stretched (up 66% in a year), making short-term mean reversion near round-number resistance highly plausible. Furthermore, I am strictly following the rule that uncertainty MUST increase with the forecast horizon. Thus, the estimates progressively decay from the 0.46 short-term anchor exactly toward 0.50 for the multi-year horizons, reflecting maximum uncertainty about long-term macro conditions and index levels after a massive rally.

The Overnight Bank Funding Rate (OBFR) closely follows the Federal Funds Rate. Based on the retrieved FRED series, OBFR has been steadily declining from 4.33 in mid-2025 down to 3.64 in April 2026, indicating an active Fed cutting cycle. Consequently, over the short-to-medium horizons (the rest of 2026), the probability of OBFR strictly increasing from its April 2026 baseline is very low, driven mainly by the potential of minor daily fluctuations. In the long term, cycles fluctuate, making distant future levels highly uncertain and naturally converging towards a base-rate like figure of 0.50.

Fannie Mae recently upwardly revised its Q2 2026 mortgage rate forecast to 6.3%, which is higher than the current index value (~6.118%). This suggests rates are likely to increase or stay elevated over the next 1-3 months (May through July 2026), peaking in Q3 before cooling. Consequently, probabilities for 1 week to 3 months are slightly above 0.5. For the 6-month to 1-year horizons, market consensus strongly expects rates to ease back down toward 5.5% to 6.0% by mid-2027, meaning a decline relative to current rates is more likely (pushing p below 0.5 for the 1-year and 3-year horizons). For the extended 5-to-10-year horizons, massive macro uncertainty forces estimates back to exactly 0.50, abiding by the instruction that uncertainty must strictly increase for distant dates.

The current VA 30-year fixed rate is at 5.871 as of late April 2026. The FRED trend model estimates a short-term drop (P=0.46 for 1-week), but Fannie Mae recently revised its conventional mortgage rate forecasts upward to 6.3% for Q2 2026 and 6.1% for the remainder of 2026 and 2027. The MBA forecasts are even higher (6.4%). Accounting for the typical ~0.25%-0.30% spread between conventional and VA rates, this implies VA rates are likely to hover near 5.8-6.0% in the medium term. This represents a rough coin flip compared to the ~5.871 anchor date value, with slight upward tilt in the 3-6 month window due to up-revised macroeconomic forecasts. For distant horizons, I drift estimates toward 0.50 as uncertainties compound.

The MBA forecasts are even higher (6.4%). Accounting for the typical ~0.25%-0.30% spread between conventional and VA rates, this implies VA rates are likely to hover near 5.8-6.0% in the medium term. This represents a rough coin flip compared to the ~5.871 anchor date value, with slight upward tilt in the 3-6 month window due to up-revised macroeconomic forecasts. For distant horizons, I drift estimates toward 0.50 as uncertainties compound.

The target value (2026-04-16 value) is exactly 0.974. Looking at the last 13 months of FRED data for this series, 0.974 is exactly the median (6 values were strictly higher, 6 strictly lower). This establishes a solid 50% baseline. However, the time series data shows significant volatility (ranging from -0.13 to +3.06) and a marked tendency to mean-revert upwards when it falls to or below ~1.0. Given that the series just fell sharply from 3.06 to 0.97, there's short-term momentum for a rebound in the near term, corroborated by the FRED tool's combined estimate of 0.63 for the coming month based on linear trend and autoregressive tendencies. I am estimating 0.60 for the 1-month horizon because of this short-term upside bias, and smoothly tapering down to 0.50 for the 10-year horizon, as long-term macroeconomic models place the natural 1-year real interest rate broadly in line with this 0.9-1.0% range, making it a pure coin flip over multidecade horizons.

The FRED series RESPPLLOPNWW (Earnings Remittances Due to the U.S. Treasury) is currently negative, representing the Federal Reserve's deferred asset generated from operating losses. After peaking in negative magnitude around -$245.9 billion in January 2026, the deferred asset has steadily shrunk to roughly -$243.0 billion by late April 2026, indicating the Fed has returned to generating positive net earnings. This structurally positive operating income is corroborated by the NY Fed and PIIE projections, which expect SOMA net income and overall profitability to resume in 2026. As the Fed generates positive income, this series becomes less negative (an increase in value). Hence, the series is highly likely to continue increasing, structurally, across all future resolution dates as the deferred asset is paid down and eventual positive remittances resume. To satisfy the mandate that uncertainty must increase with the forecast horizon, the submitted probabilities feature a mathematically strict monotonic decrease towards 0.5 over successive time horizons.

The question anchors on the value of the FRED daily series RPONTSYD on 2026-04-26. Since April 26 is a Sunday, the series likely won't have a new entry and will either revert to 0 or use the previous business day's value (2026-04-24), which is historically confirmed to be 0.0. Overnight Fed repurchase operations have generally been strictly 0 under the abundant reserves framework, though small 'test' operations (like 0.001 billion) or mid-month tax-day spikes (like April 15) occur occasionally. Looking at the sampled data, less than a third of the observed trading days feature a positive volume >0. If the baseline anchor is 0.0, the chance of an increase on any of the target dates is exactly the chance that the Fed conducts >$0 in temporary OMO repos on those dates. Based on recent frequency, this is around 20-35%. As the forecast horizon extends to the late 2020s and 2030s, the probability regressions smoothly towards the maximum uncertainty point (0.50) due to ambiguity in future Federal Reserve operating frameworks and reserve conditions.

The FRED tool initially suggested a combined probability of 61% for an increase, relying on generic trend models. However, taking a closer look at the actual series dynamics reveals a much more specific situational advantage: the most recent data point (April 24, 2026) is 0.082 billion, which is an unusually deep minimum in an otherwise highly sporadic regime. During "empty facility" periods (like late 2019 and early 2026), the overnight reverse repo frequently bounces between 0.100 and 1.000 billion due to residual counterparty usage. Analyzing sample data from late 2019, approximately 85% of days in that structurally constrained regime exceeded 0.082. Consequently, simply due to random day-to-day positive swings above this near-zero floor, any given near-term date is highly likely to record an amount strictly greater than 0.082.

For the nearest weekend dates like Sunday, May 3, standard resolution protocols historically evaluate against the nearest published proxy (May 1, a Friday post-month-end, which favors an elevated reading). The most substantial risks for a "No" resolution are if daily volume structurally freezes exactly at $0.00 or if the dataset is altered or deprecated in the future. Because policy frameworks (such as the Fed’s floor system) and macroeconomic environments (like quantitative easing/tightening) are highly uncertain over multi-year spans, confidence gracefully declines, pulling probabilities symmetrically toward 0.50 (50%) for the longer-range targets out to 2036.

Consequently, simply due to random day-to-day positive swings above this near-zero floor, any given near-term date is highly likely to record an amount strictly greater than 0.082.

For the nearest weekend dates like Sunday, May 3, standard resolution protocols historically evaluate against the nearest published proxy (May 1, a Friday post-month-end, which favors an elevated reading). The most substantial risks for a "No" resolution are if daily volume structurally freezes exactly at $0.00 or if the dataset is altered or deprecated in the future. Because policy frameworks (such as the Fed’s floor system) and macroeconomic environments (like quantitative easing/tightening) are highly uncertain over multi-year spans, confidence gracefully declines, pulling probabilities symmetrically toward 0.50 (50%) for the longer-range targets out to 2036.

The FRED tool initially suggested a combined probability of 61% for an increase, relying on generic trend models. However, taking a closer look at the actual series dynamics reveals a much more specific situational advantage: the most recent data point (April 24, 2026) is 0.082 billion, which is an unusually deep minimum in an otherwise highly sporadic regime. The target variable is a 180-day moving average of daily SOFR. From the data retrieved, the 180-day average has been dropping steadily by about 0.003-0.004 per day due to older, higher rates (from Oct/Nov 2025, which were around 4.35%) dropping out of the 180-day window and being replaced by current daily SOFR rates, which are around 3.6% (tied to the 3.5-3.75% Fed Funds target). Because the current daily rate is substantially lower than the 180-day average (currently ~3.789%), this moving average is mathematically guaranteed to continue falling over the immediate short-term (1-3 months). For the 180-day average to increase above today's value by 2026-10-23 (6 months from now), the Fed would have to hike rates well above 3.8% soon, which is highly unlikely given current market expectations and Fed language projecting 'fewer cuts' and neutral pacing, rather than immediate hikes. Over the 1-year horizon to 2027, the chances of a rate hike cycle are somewhat broader, but structural expectations for the FFR are still biased downward. For the very long term (2029 to 2036), there is greater uncertainty, and baseline expectations of neutral rates generally lean lower (~2.5-3%), making it slightly less than a coin-toss that rates rebound solidly above 3.8%. Therefore, near-term probabilities are exceptionally low, appropriately scaling up closer to 0.5 over 10-year forecasts.

The 10-year breakeven inflation rate typically hovers around 2.3% as long-term market expectations are anchored to the Fed's 2% PCE target (which translates to ~2.3% CPI, the index used for TIPS). As of April 24, 2026, the breakeven rate is 2.42%, up from 2.38% a few days earlier. Because 2.42% is at the higher end of the recent historical range (since late 2024, it has stayed between 2.2% and 2.42%), mean reversion represents a significant downward pull on future estimates relative to the 2.42% current baseline. The FRED statistical tool yields an initial projection of 0.32 probability for an increase by early May. Even over extended horizons (up to 10 years), the historical unconditional mean (~2.3%) is below the current anchor of 2.42%, so the long-term probability of being above 2.42% converges to the upper tail of the steady-state distribution, which is around 0.42 to 0.45. Therefore, the probability should start low (0.32) reflecting immediate mean reversion prospects, and gradually increase toward the unconditional base expectation (0.45) for the longest horizons.

The VIX value on 2026-04-23 (the latest available prior to 04-26, assuming weekend) was approximately 19.31. This is above the long-term historical median of approximately 17.5-18.0. VIX is characterized by sharp spikes followed by relatively rapid mean reversion (historically dropping ~4 points on average from typical crisis peaks within a few weeks). Since the VIX recently spiked to ~25 in early April and is currently reverting, the expectation for the near term (1 week to 1 month out) is a continued mean reversion toward its baseline of 13-16, making the likelihood of it being higher than ~19 slightly lower than 50% (estimated at 43-44%). Over the long term (years ahead), the probability of the VIX exceeding a random value of ~19 on any given date converges to the long-term stationary distribution, where values > 19 occur about 40-45% of the time, given the right-skewed nature of the index. In accordance with forecasting principles, uncertainty has been modeled to increase gradually with time, moving the probability uniformly toward 0.50 for the most distant resolution dates (2031 and 2036).

The initial time-series analysis for the Federal Reserve liquidity facility loans (WLCFLL) shows recent weekly fluctuations between roughly $3.5B and $9B, with the April 22, 2026 value at $6073M. The historical context reflects a wind-down of legacy emergency facilities like the BTFP, which officially ceased new loans in Mar 2024 and matured by Mar 2025. The current $6000M baseline represents regular discount window lending or residual activities, and $6073M sits toward the higher end of the most recent 3-month range (which averaged closer to $4800M). The FRED trend logic gives a short-term probability of an increase around 0.34, owing to recent negative momentum and mean reversion from the localized peak. Because the standard 'normal' usage of Fed lending facilities tends to be quite low, any significant continuous increase usually requires liquidity stresses. In a mean-reverting environment without immediate crisis, short-term probabilities lean against an increase (0.35-0.41). Over the mid-to-long term (3-10 years), uncertainty grows. Nominal macroeconomic growth naturally increases the dollar values of stress episodes, drifting baseline probabilities back toward ~0.50 as unpredictability maxes out over a 10-year horizon.

The baseline comparison value for this question will be the WLODLL reading for the week ending near 2026-04-26. The latest observed data point for 2026-04-22 reveals a sharp decline in depository institution reserves to roughly 2.915 trillion. This significant drop from earlier in April (>3.18 trillion) maps directly to the usual tax-season drain, where funds are transferred to the Treasury General Account (TGA) around the mid-April tax deadline. Because the baseline value is taken at or near this seasonal trough, there is a strong probability of the series increasing in the short-term as normal government disbursements resume and TGA balances normalize, returning funds to bank reserves. 

The FRED tool's combined trend and seasonal estimate suggests a 70% probability of an increase by the first resolution date. Early to mid-summer dates (May, July) maintain quite high likelihoods of exceeding the April tax trough. Beyond the one-year horizon, long-term macroeconomic and monetary factors (like changing deposit footprints and shifting quantitative tightening/easing policies) dominate. Over extended periods, forecasting exact economic aggregates carries progressively higher fundamental uncertainty. Therefore, directly following the strict instruction that "uncertainty should INCREASE with forecast horizon," the probabilities are scheduled to monotonically decay toward 0.5 for the distant, multi-year resolution dates.

Early to mid-summer dates (May, July) maintain quite high likelihoods of exceeding the April tax trough. Beyond the one-year horizon, long-term macroeconomic and monetary factors (like changing deposit footprints and shifting quantitative tightening/easing policies) dominate. Over extended periods, forecasting exact economic aggregates carries progressively higher fundamental uncertainty. Therefore, directly following the strict instruction that "uncertainty should INCREASE with forecast horizon," the probabilities are scheduled to monotonically decay toward 0.5 for the distant, multi-year resolution dates.

The data shows a consistent recent positive trend for M1 (WM1NS) at about +2.86 per day since late 2025. This supports a >0.5 probability for most future dates. However, considering intra-month seasonality, M1 tends to be higher at the beginning of the month (e.g., May 3) and can dip toward the end of the month (May 26), which was observed when late May 2025 dipped slightly below late April 2025. Thus, the 1-month forecast (May 26) is given a slightly lower/borderline probability, while the 1-week and 3-month forecasts are stronger. Following the strict instruction that uncertainty must increase with forecast horizon, the probabilities for long-term dates are systematically pulled closer to 0.5.

The base date for comparison is late April (2026-04-26), which intrinsically correlates with the annual seasonal low of Not Seasonally Adjusted M2 (WM2NS) due to Mid-April tax payments draining bank deposits into the Treasury General Account. The first resolution date is early May (2026-05-03). In early May, M2 always experiences a robust bounce due to massive beginning-of-month government benefit distributions (Social Security, salaries, etc.). This makes the immediate short-term probability of an increase very high. The FRED tool confirmed recent upward momentum. Following the strictly mandated instruction that 'uncertainty should INCREASE with forecast horizon — probabilities for distant dates should be closer to 0.5', the probabilities gracefully taper down from a high initial conviction regarding the specific early-May seasonal bounce toward an anchor of ~0.50 for far-distant horizons.

The most recent data point for April 22, 2026 is roughly $2.915 trillion. This reflects a sharp seasonal dip caused by mid-April tax payments draining reserves into the Treasury General Account (TGA) (down from $3.18T on April 8). Given that QT officially ended in late 2025 and the Fed has explicitly transitioned to an "ample reserves" framework (estimated by analysts at roughly $2.7T-$3.0T), the current level of ~$2.9T represents a likely local minimum. As the Treasury spends down the tax influx, reserves will naturally bounce back above $3.0 Trillion over the successive weeks and months. The Fed has also been conducting Reserve Management Purchases (RMPs) to offset balance sheet shrinking and maintain these required levels. Therefore, there is a very high probability that reserves will increase in the short term relative to this late-April trough. I follow the prompt's instructions strictly, smoothly decreasing absolute confidence toward 0.5 as the forecast horizon extends to decades out, leading to my final monotonic probability curve.

The Federal Reserve officially ended overall balance sheet runoff in late 2025, but its specific policy for Mortgage-Backed Securities (MBS) is to reinvest all maturity proceeds into Treasury bills. This guarantees that zero new MBS are being purchased, meaning the MBS portfolio will continuously shrink by approximately $15-20 billion per month due to natural maturities and prepayments. Reaching the ~$1.993 Trillion mark recorded in late April 2026 would require a massive, new round of Quantitative Easing (QE) explicitly buying MBS, which the Fed strongly opposes acting on in the long run (they prefer a Treasuries-only portfolio).

In the very short term (weeks to months), the trend is monotonic reduction, aside from exceedingly rare, tiny settlement blips (e.g., +$17 million in Feb 2026), but because the resolution needs a strict INCREASE over the baseline, short-term probabilities are essentially zero. Uncertainty rationally climbs for longer horizons (2031, 2036), where another major economic crisis like 2008 or 2020 could potentially force an unexpected return to massive MBS QE resulting in a balance surpassing the 2026 baseline.

The forecasting date is April 26, 2026, meaning there are only roughly 8 months left until the January 1, 2027 deadline. Search results from late 2025 and early 2026 show that Huawei remains steadfastly opposed to Open RAN. Huawei is characterized in a February 2026 article as a "vocal opponent" of including mandatory Open RAN specifications in 6G standards. They have allowed themselves to be disqualified from European operator tenders (e.g., Deutsche Telekom's 30,000-site Open RAN transition in late 2025) rather than build O-RAN specs into their products. They remain entirely outside the O-RAN Alliance. While German regulations aim to mandate independent network management elements by 2029, operators like Deutsche Telekom are explicitly writing their own software to interface with Huawei's closed platforms rather than Huawei releasing its own Open RAN products. Given Huawei's firm strategic dismissal of Open RAN and the very short timeframe remaining, the probability of them introducing or announcing a commercial Open RAN equipment offering before the end of the year is exceptionally low, consistent with the crowd estimate of 2%.

China currently has only one overseas military base (in Djibouti) and the process of establishing new bases has historically been slow due to a mix of diplomatic caution and host nation sovereignty concerns. While US intelligence and numerous reports have noted suspected Chinese intentions in African countries such as Equatorial Guinea and Gabon, open-source searches through late April 2026 show no concrete evidence of confirmed site agreements or commencement of construction anywhere in Africa. Recent analyses (early 2026) still refer to these bases as 'rumored' and note that any host nation faces significant diplomatic hurdles, particularly heavy pushback from the United States. With just 8 months remaining until the January 1, 2027 resolution date, the window for an agreement to become public and for ground to be broken (or formal conversion of a dual-use port for PLA use to be declared) is very narrow. Even a rapid transformation of an existing facility like the Port of Bata would require an unmistakable public shift in usage or an official announcement, which seems extremely unlikely given current geopolitical posturing. The baseline probability is thus very low, estimated at roughly 5%.

The ambition to build a synthetic cell from the bottom up is being actively pursued by major initiatives, such as the EVOLF program (a newly funded 2024–2034 Dutch initiative succeeding BaSyC). However, the resolution criteria for this question are remarkably restrictive: the cell must be constructed *entirely* from chemically synthesized components rather than being extracted from naturally occurring cells. It must also feature active metabolism and self-replication independently. Currently, state-of-the-art bottom-up designs heavily rely on biological extracts (like the PURE system) for intricate machinery like the ribosome. Generating a completely synthetic alternative or building complex biological replicators strictly through chemical synthesis (without natural biological intermediaries) by 2035 represents an extremely large technological leap. The 2035 timeline maps exactly to the end of the new 10-year EVOLF program, but grand challenge synthetic biology timelines historically slip, and hitting every one of these strict criteria without compromise is highly improbable in the next nine years. Therefore, I agree with the market base rate around 13-15%.

The question asks whether mirror biology research will be classified under dual-use oversight by the US, EU, or UK by the end of 2030. The requirements explicitly include additions to DURC, UKRI/Horizon Europe funding requirements, or dual-use export controls. The background indicates mirror biology is hypothetically extremely dangerous (immune evasion, environmental disruption). Evidence retrieved from 2024–2026 shows a powerful, rapid mobilization of experts asking for a moratorium. Notably:
1. In late 2024, dozens of world-leading scientists published a call in *Science* for a halt on mirror life research, explicitly urging funders not to support it. 
2. The UK Government Office for Science held an expert roundtable recommending the prevention of mirror organism development. 
3. UNESCO’s International Bioethics Committee is backing a global moratorium. 
4. Germany’s central bio-safety committee (ZKBS) issued a formal warning. 
5. Private funders (like the Sloan Foundation) have already instituted formal funding bans. 

Regulatory agencies usually move slowly, but this is an instance where the scientific community and initial government reviews (UK and Germany) are strongly and proactively pushing for 'red lines' on funding/oversight BEFORE the technology matures. Given that there are nearly 5 years left until the December 2030 deadline—and that only ONE jurisdiction (US, EU, or UK) needs to formally implement oversight—the likelihood of at least one public funding body (e.g., UKRI, Horizon Europe, or US DURC) adding mirror biology to its dual-use restrictions is high. The goal is the complete synthesis and assembly of a 70S ribosome using strictly recombinant proteins and in vitro transcribed (IVT) rRNA by 2030. The 30S subunit has already been fully synthesized in vitro (2020). For the 50S subunit, two major components have been independently verified: (1) Researchers successfully assembled E. coli 70S ribosomes using a completely recombinant set of ribosomal proteins in 2022, proving the protein side is solvable. (2) Functional 50S subunits have been successfully reconstituted with IVT 23S rRNA in the past (e.g., using B. stearothermophilus rRNA, or using E. coli rRNA with osmolytes to stimulate folding as shown by Semrad & Green 2002). 

The remaining gap is to combine these fully recombinant proteins with IVT rRNA in a defined in vitro system (like a PURE system, possibly supplemented with recombinant assembly factors like EngA or ObgE, which are actively being studied as of a 2026 preprint) and demonstrate measurable peptidyl transferase activity. Since "measurable peptidyl transferase activity" is a relatively modest functional threshold (compared to high-yield full protein translation), and well-funded groups like Ueda's at RIKEN and Jewett's are actively publishing major milestones every 2-3 years, there is a high likelihood (around 65%) that they will cross this final integration hurdle within the 4.5 years before 2030.

Considering the structural advantage of an open seat, I estimate the true probability favors the Democrats slightly more than Kalshi's 51% but perhaps less than Metaculus' 61%. I anchor at 60%, with decreasing confidence (closer to 50%) for the longer-term horizons as instructed.

The 2021 Australian Census recorded 38.9% of the population as having 'No Religion'. To reach at least 50% in 2026, an absolute increase of 11.1 percentage points is required. Historically, the share grew by 7.8% from 2011-2016, and by 8.8% from 2016-2021 (accelerating by 1.0%). Reaching 50% would require the growth rate to accelerate by 2.3% over the prior period, meaning the absolute number of non-religious people would have to jump by roughly 44% in 5 years. Two primary factors make this overperformance unlikely: 

1) Question format retention: While secular activists successfully lobbied the ABS to test a two-part format ("Does the person have a religion? Yes/No"), the ABS officially confirmed in early 2025 that it will retain the traditional format ("What is the person's religion?") for the 2026 census to maintain historical comparability. This format suffers from acquiescence bias, meaning an artificial methodological surge in 'No Religion' responses will not occur. 
2) Record Immigration: Following border reopenings, Australia saw record net overseas migration from 2022-2024 (mostly from countries with high religiosity like India and the Philippines). The denominator of the population will grow significantly, diluting the native generational replacement trend. 

Without a change in the census question, demographic trends alone are projected to land 'No Religion' in the 47%-49% range. Hitting 50.0% requires a historically unprecedented statistical acceleration that demographic projections (such as logistic trend lines) do not currently support. I am confident in placing the probability around 26%.

The community prediction recently stands at 22%. Current policy pathways from the 2024 UNEP Emissions Gap Report project a central estimate around 2.9°C of warming by 2100, with a 37% chance of exceeding 3.0°C. The threshold for this question effectively requires hitting approximately 3.4°C above the 1951-1980 baseline. A 5-year average incorporates natural variability, which can push the temperature 0.1-0.2°C above the climate trendline, making the required trendline temperature somewhat lower (~3.2-3.3°C). Achieving this requires either emissions consistently missing targets (e.g., SSP3-7.0 scenarios) or climate sensitivity being in the upper end of the distribution (a "fat tail" risk). Both are plausible, yielding an estimated probability in the 20-25% range, well-aligned with the community consensus of 22%. I have provided slightly increasing probabilities to satisfy the literal prompt requirement of increasing uncertainty for the parsed resolution date components.

The 2023 Fiscal Responsibility Act imposed NEPA deadlines (1 year for EAs, 2 years for EISs) but failed to satisfy the question's requirement that timeline extensions require the "agreement of the project applicant" (the FRA only required "consultation"). In December 2025, the House passed the SPEED Act—mostly along party lines (221-196)—which explicitly includes the necessary applicant agreement stipulation. No multi-year mortality trials are occurring, and recent PRISMA systematic reviews explicitly limit their scope on longevity claims to preclinical (mouse) models. 
4. **Conclusion**: Given the rigid timeframe and exact phrasing required by the resolution criteria ("explicitly mention a lifespan improvement of at least 5% in humans"), this resolves negatively barring an exceptionally loose and unorthodox phrasing in a future low-quality review. Probability builds slowly over the horizon but remains near the lower bound.

The Swedish Social Democratic Party (S) has been the largest party in every general election since 1914. Recent 2025 and 2026 polling aggregate data shows them maintaining a commanding lead, currently polling around 34-35%. Their closest rival, the Sweden Democrats (SD), is polling around 21-22%. With an election 5 months away and an estimated 13-point lead, a reversal is historically unprecedented and highly unlikely, especially given the current government's struggles with the economy. The Polymarket price of 0.89 appropriately prices in a minor tail risk, but true odds are realistically north of 90%. Probabilities slightly decrease to account for distant horizons.

Brazilian hemorrhagic fever is an extremely rare disease caused by the Sabiá virus, with only a few cases ever recorded since its discovery in the 1990s. Because of its rarity, there is virtually no commercial incentive to develop a vaccine, and clinical efficacy trials would be virtually impossible to conduct in humans. While it is considered a potential bioweapon agent (meaning some biodefense research could theoretically happen), there are no known ongoing clinical trials or active advanced development programs for a Sabiá virus vaccine. 

For the short-term resolution dates (2026-2027), the probability of a vaccine being successfully created and formally recognized on Wikipedia is effectively zero, bounded only by the 0.05 minimum requested by the system. For the longer-term dates extending into the 2030s, there is a slight chance that a pan-arenavirus vaccine, a biodefense initiative utilizing rapid mRNA platforms, or a preclinical vaccine candidate incorrectly classified as a human vaccine on Wikipedia might cause the resolution criteria to evaluate to 'Yes'. Consequently, the probability slowly rises to 0.12 for the 2036 resolution date.

Nepomniachtchi's rating as of mid-April 2026 is roughly 2729. A 1% increase requires him to achieve an official FIDE classical rating of roughly 2757 (a ~28 point jump). Over short timescales (May 3, May 26), this is essentially impossible given that FIDE lists are updated monthly, his April schedule was dominated by Freestyle (Chess960) rather than classical events, and gaining 28 points in a single month at the super-GM level is extremely rare. For near-term dates, I assign 0.05 (the lowest allowed bound in guidelines). Although Nepo's historical peak is 2795, meaning he functionally has the capability to be >2756, he has been in a slump (briefly falling out of the top 20 earlier in 2026). Over medium horizons (late 2026 to 2027), there's a modest chance he recovers his form, so probability rises to ~0.08 - 0.12. In the long term (2029-2036), he will be well into his late 30s and mid 40s, a period when ratings typically decline due to age. However, per the instruction to widen uncertainty for distant dates toward 0.5, probabilities are gradually scaled up to 0.38 for 2036.

The empirical base rate for vaccine development questions resolving YES is ~0.000, setting a very low prior. We know that as of April 16, 2026, the Wikipedia page says 'No' for a Trichuriasis (whipworm) vaccine. A web search reveals that despite active research (with a promising 'Tm-WAP' protein candidate for mucosal vaccination identified recently), no human trials for a whipworm vaccine have even started. In fact, an ongoing 2024-2025 Phase 1 trial (NCT05706116) isn't even testing a vaccine; it's simply establishing a Controlled Human Infection Model (CHIM) by evaluating the safety of experimentally infecting people with Trichuris trichiura eggs to test FUTURE vaccines or treatments. Given that establishing a safe human challenge model usually takes a few years, and any potential vaccine candidate would then have to go through Phase 1, 2, and 3 clinical trials (which typically take 8-15 years for novel vaccines without an active pandemic-level acceleration), the probability of finding a viable candidate, completing all trials, gaining regulatory approval, and being added to Wikipedia by 2029 is virtually zero. For the 2031 date, probabilities remain very low given the poor funding and historically slow progress for neglected tropical disease vaccines. By 2036, there is a small chance (10-15%) that early Phase 1 candidates currently in preclinical trials using the new infection model could reach the market. The probabilities must be bounded strictly above zero per the rules, so I am assigning 0.05 to the nearest dates and increasing slightly for the furthest date.

Humpy is currently ranked #5 on the April 2026 FIDE women's list with a rating of 2535. Her position is extremely fragile because she is tied on rating with Tan Zhongyi (#6) and only 1 point ahead of Aleksandra Goryachkina (#7). However, the major classical event in April 2026 was the Women's Candidates Tournament, where evidence suggests Goryachkina struggled and lost points, and lower-rated players like Vaishali won, absorbing those points. Without other young players gaining enough to surpass 2535 immediately, Humpy is highly likely to retain a top-5 spot on the rapidly approaching May 1st rating list (which covers both May resolution dates). For subsequent dates, her likelihood of remaining top 5 decreases significantly: she is 39 years old, relatively less active, and multiple younger players (Assaubayeva, Divya Deshmukh, Vaishali) are climbing steadily. I searched both general and clinical trial databases for a vaccine against Legionella pneumophila, which causes Legionnaires' disease and Pontiac fever. No vaccines are currently approved, and there are completely zero vaccine candidates in clinical trials on ClinicalTrials.gov. Current research remains largely in prehistoric / pre-clinical stages using vaccinomics, animal models, or computational designs. As vaccine development from preclinical stages to clinical availability reliably takes 10+ years (barring extreme emergencies, which Pontiac fever is certainly not as it is a generally mild and self-limiting illness and prevention focuses instead on environmental management of water systems), it is virtually impossible that a vaccine will be approved and incorporated into the Wikipedia reference page within the next 5 years (prob bounded by the prompt's 0.05 lower limit). Even looking ahead 10 years to 2036, without a current Phase 1 candidate and given the low commercial drive for a vaccine against this pathogen, approval scenarios remain highly unlikely, justifying a low long-term probability of 0.15.

There is currently no FDA-approved vaccine for human metapneumovirus (hMPV), and the Wikipedia page 'List of infectious diseases' leaves the vaccine field empty as of early 2026. However, there are significant clinical trial efforts currently underway. Most notably, AstraZeneca acquired Icosavax in February 2024 for a "Phase III-ready" bivalent RSV and hMPV vaccine (IVX-A12). Another major player is Moderna, developing an mRNA vaccine (mRNA-1653) which recently published Phase 1b data in 2025. Given AstraZeneca's resources and the Fast Track designation IVX-A12 holds, it is highly likely that Phase III trials are ongoing or recently completed over the 2024-2025/2026 respiratory seasons. An FDA approval for AstraZeneca’s vaccine could plausibly happen between late 2026 and early 2027. Because we are at the forecast date (April 2026) and no approval has been announced yet, probabilities for mid-2026 remain very low. However, probabilities rise sharply for early 2027 as Phase 3 data reads out and BLAs are processed. Over a 3-10 year horizon, the intense effort by multiple large pharma companies (including mRNA combination vaccines) makes it extremely likely a successful candidate will be approved and subsequently added to Wikipedia.

Kateryna Lagno's April 2026 rating was 2508. A 1% increase requires her to reach ~2533. She recently played in the 2026 FIDE Women's Candidates Tournament (March-April 2026), where she finished with 6.5/14. As this is below 50% against players with a similar average rating, she will have lost a few rating points or stayed essentially flat, placing her live rating somewhere around 2503-2508. Given FIDE rating updates monthly, it is virtually impossible for her rating to jump to 2533+ for the May 2026 lists (resolving the first two dates). In the short-to-medium term (2026-2027), gaining 25-30 Elo at the top level is extremely difficult without dominant long-term performances, supported by the very low empirical base rate of 0.007 for +1% rating questions. As she gets older (born 1989), returning to a >2533 rating gets harder due to natural decline, although systemic rating inflation might theoretically play a role over longer horizons (2031, 2036). My probabilities follow the instruction to keep uncertainty low for near-term knowns and increase uncertainty towards 0.5 for the far future.

Her rating on the forecast date (April 2026) is 2534. A 1% increase requires an Elo of 2560. She recently scored 7.5/14 in the FIDE Women's Candidates Tournament (March/April 2026). Given this is only slightly above 50% against a 2500+ field, her rating will change minimally (estimated +2 to 2536). Therefore, it is virtually impossible for her rating to hit 2560 on the May 1 FIDE list, leaving the May 3 and May 26 probabilities at the minimum (0.02). Over the summer and fall of 2026, gaining 24-26 points requires a series of exceptional performances (+2.5 wins above expected), which is unlikely but possible, leading to low probabilities for July and October. However, her peak rating was 2611 in 2021, and she has historically spent a lot of time over 2560. It is not an unprecedented high for her, simply a regression to her past mean. This gives much higher probabilities for 2027 and beyond than the 0.007 empirical base rate suggests. In accordance with the instruction, uncertainty increases toward 0.5 for distant horizons (2029, 2031, 2036).

Desmodesmus infection is an extremely rare opportunistic infection caused by a green alga (Desmodesmus armatus). There is no public health, scientific, or economic rationale to develop a vaccine for such a localized, rare pathogen. Given the lack of any clinical trials, research, or conceivable need, the probability of a vaccine being successfully developed, approved, and added to the 'List of infectious diseases' Wikipedia article by any of the resolution dates is virtually zero. However, per the instructions to bound predictions between 0.05 and 0.95 and to increase uncertainty over the long term (such as potential broad-spectrum breakthroughs or Wikipedia vandalism), the probabilities map to the lowest permitted values, gradually increasing slightly for distant forecast horizons.

As of April 2026, there is no vaccine available for Ehrlichiosis. The Wikipedia page 'List of infectious diseases' does not list any. Literature searches indicate that vaccine candidates for Ehrlichiosis are still in the preclinical stages (e.g., structure-based vaccines and multi-epitope peptide vaccines). Since clinical trials typically take many years and have high failure rates, development of a vaccine before 2030 is virtually impossible (Probability ~0.02-0.03). Expected time to a developed and approved vaccine would exceed 10 years, giving only a minor chance (15%) by 2036. The step-wise probabilities follow an increasing uncertainty with distant horizons.

Lagno's ranking on April 1 (and thus the knowledge cutoff date of April 26) was 11. Thus, "as high or higher" means a rank of 1 through 11. 

In the April 2026 FIDE Women's Candidates Tournament, Lagno played 14 rounds against highly-rated peers. Analysis of her results shows she finished with 6.5/14 (she scored 5.0 in the first 10 rounds, drew Goryachkina, defeated Deshmukh, and lost to Assaubayeva and Vaishali). Because the field's average rating was extremely high (~2520) and her rating was 2508, a 6.5 score yields a tiny rating loss, bringing her from 2508 down slightly to ~2505.

Crucially, Divya Deshmukh (Rank 10) had a disastrous tournament, scoring only 5.0/14. As a result, Deshmukh predictably loses over 15 points, dropping her to ~2493. Because Deshmukh drops significantly below Lagno's new rating of 2505, and because no one from behind mathematically passed 2505 (the next highest inactive player, Shuvalova, is at 2502; Vaishali gained massive ground but only rose to ~2495), Lagno is overwhelmingly likely to improve her rank to #10 on the May 2026 list, or remain exactly #11. Either way, she will be ranked as high or higher (<= 11) for the short-term May resolution dates. 

Over the medium-to-long term, Lagno is 36 years old. While she retains world-class strength today, historical precedent in women's chess strongly suggests an inevitable rank drop out of the top 11 as she ages into her late 30s and 40s (1 to 10 years out), and as fast-rising young talents (like Deshmukh, Assaubayeva, Vaishali, and Zhu Jiner) overtake her. Therefore, the probability naturally steps downward over time, eventually dropping below 50% for horizons of 3 to 10 years away. The longest dates use estimates closer to 0.5 out of an abundance of epistemic caution regarding forecasting events 5-10 years in the future.

Because Deshmukh drops significantly below Lagno's new rating of 2505, and because no one from behind mathematically passed 2505 (the next highest inactive player, Shuvalova, is at 2502; Vaishali gained massive ground but only rose to ~2495), Lagno is overwhelmingly likely to improve her rank to #10 on the May 2026 list, or remain exactly #11. Either way, she will be ranked as high or higher (<= 11) for the short-term May resolution dates. 

Over the medium-to-long term, Lagno is 36 years old. While she retains world-class strength today, historical precedent in women's chess strongly suggests an inevitable rank drop out of the top 11 as she ages into her late 30s and 40s (1 to 10 years out), and as fast-rising young talents (like Deshmukh, Assaubayeva, Vaishali, and Zhu Jiner) overtake her. Therefore, the probability naturally steps downward over time, eventually dropping below 50% for horizons of 3 to 10 years away. The longest dates use estimates closer to 0.5 out of an abundance of epistemic caution regarding forecasting events 5-10 years in the future.

The condition "Food poisoning by Clostridium perfringens" is a very mild, self-limiting illness and an extremely poor target for human vaccine development. Extensive searching reveals no human clinical trials for a vaccine against this pathogen; most research is at the in-silico stage, in mice models, or focused on veterinary diseases (like necrotic enteritis in poultry) or gas gangrene. Furthermore, the Wikipedia page "List of infectious diseases" is notoriously incomplete, leaving the vaccine column blank even for diseases with universally recognized vaccines (e.g., Chickenpox, Hepatitis A/B). Thus, even if a vaccine were to enter trials, the likelihood that it reaches the market and is specifically updated in this Wikipedia table's cell is vanishingly small. Probabilities are kept at the absolute minimum for short-to-medium horizons and rise only fractionally for the 5-10 year horizons to account for deep uncertainty or hypothetical multi-pathogen mRNA vaccine rollouts that might theoretically warrant a Wikipedia update.

No vaccines are currently in development for human bocavirus. HBoV typically causes mild respiratory symptoms in children and lacks strong commercial R&D interest compared to other respiratory viruses like RSV. In previous searches, authoritative medical sources point out the lack of any vaccine in development for human use. Typical vaccine development timelines span 10+ years from initial preclinical research to approval. Given the absolute absence of even preclinical candidates as of 2026, the probability of a vaccine being fully developed and listed on Wikipedia by 2026-2027 is effectively nil, and remains extremely low through 2036. The slight increase in probability over time primarily reflects general uncertainty over longer time horizons (such as the possibility of a rapidly-developed pan-viral mRNA vaccine or unpredicted breakthrough in virology).

The development of a scabies vaccine is currently only in the pre-clinical stage (rabbit models) as of 2024-2025. Given that typical vaccine development timelines from pre-clinical trials to approval extend well over 5-10 years, it is highly unlikely that a vaccine will be developed and listed on Wikipedia by 2026 or 2027. The probability slightly increases for the distant 2031 and 2036 dates to account for potential long-term medical breakthroughs, though scabies remains a somewhat neglected disease, implying funding and fast-tracked trials are improbable. As the resolution criteria depend on Wikipedia lists, there is also a negligible chance of vandalism, but it would typically be reverted.

Heartland virus is an extremely rare emerging tick-borne disease with fewer than 100 documented cases in the US. There are currently no vaccines available, and a web search reveals only early-stage, pre-clinical work (e.g., using reverse genetics to explore potential live-attenuated approaches). Developing a vaccine from pre-clinical stage to clinical approval typically takes 10+ years, and given the extremely small number of annual cases, there is little to no commercial incentive or feasible path for conventional efficacy trials (Phase 3). While the FDA's Animal Rule could theoretically be used, it would still require significant dedicated biodefense or public health funding, which currently favors more prevalent or severe pathogens. As a result, the probability of an approved or widely recognized 'created' vaccine being listed on Wikipedia remains extremely low across the entire 10-year forecast horizon, constrained primarily by the base rate of Wikipedia table additions for unapproved candidate vaccines.

The Wikipedia list currently states "No" for the Helicobacter pylori vaccine. Thorough searches of the current literature from 2024 to early 2026 reveal that although there is highly active preclinical development (computational models, mouse models, mRNA candidates, multi-epitope designs), there are currently no active advanced-stage human clinical trials. A widely cited 2015 Phase 3 trial in China appears to have failed to show durable, long-term efficacy and did not lead to an approved vaccine, as supported by a March 2026 'npj Vaccines' paper stating "no vaccine has yet demonstrated durable efficacy in clinical settings." Given the roughly 5-10 year timeline required to move a vaccine from preclinical stages through Phase 1-3 trials and final regulatory approval, an approved vaccine is virtually impossible in the short term (2026-2027). The probability remains very low even by 2031. Over a full decade (by 2036), there is a modest chance that one of the current preclinical candidates might successfully navigate clinical trials and be approved, so the probability increases to 0.15 for the most distant resolution date.

- For May 2026 (both May 3 and May 26 dates), the probability is near identical to 0 (minimum 0.02 limit applied).
- For July and October 2026, the probability remains very low, as gaining 25 Elo points requires stringing consecutive major tournament wins, which happens rarely (0.03-0.04).
- Over longer horizons (2027-2036), the probability scales up toward 0.5 because structural FIDE changes, prolonged rating inflation, or a resurgence in performance becomes less predictable. However, given her age in 2036 (she will be 46), her overall expectation remains a slow Elo decay, preventing probabilities from moving entirely to 0.5.

Baylisascariasis is a very rare infection in humans (fewer than 100 documented cases in the US), making it a neglected disease with virtually zero commercial incentive for human vaccine development. While there is early-stage research on a vaccine for giant pandas against *B. schroederi*, progressing from mouse models to an approved veterinary vaccine takes many years. Even if a veterinary vaccine were developed, it is uncertain whether it would be listed on Wikipedia's 'List of infectious diseases', which primarily focuses on human medicine. Therefore, the probability of a vaccine being listed as 'Yes' in the short term is set to the minimum allowable bound of 0.05. It slowly increases to 0.12 by 2036 to reflect growing uncertainty over a 10-year horizon, accounting for the possibility of long-term veterinary breakthroughs combined with corresponding, albeit unlikely, Wikipedia edits.

There is currently no approved vaccine for African sleeping sickness (African trypanosomiasis). Searches confirm that there are no vaccine candidates for this disease currently in human clinical trials. The development of a vaccine against Trypanosoma brucei has been notoriously difficult for over a century due to the parasite's adeptness at immune evasion through variant surface glycoproteins (VSGs). While some preclinical work involving invariant antigens or DNA vaccines is ongoing, standard vaccine development from preclinical stages to regulatory approval typically takes at least 10 years. Furthermore, the development of new, highly effective oral therapies (like fexinidazole) and strong vector control have put the disease on a path toward elimination, reducing the economic and public health urgency for a novel vaccine. Thus, the probability of a vaccine being created and recognized on Wikipedia in the near term (2026-2029) is extremely low. The probability rises slightly for the 2031 and 2036 horizons given the potential for long-term technological breakthroughs, but remains low overall.

The development of a Group A streptococcus (GAS) vaccine has historically been challenged by scientific hurdles and safety concerns (specifically, fear of triggering autoimmune responses like rheumatic fever). Our searches confirm that as of early 2026, the global pipeline for a GAS vaccine remains in early clinical development. For example, GSK recently initiated a Phase 1 clinical trial in mid-2025, and other peptide or multivalent candidates (like J8-KS42 and StreptAnova) have either recently completed or are undergoing early-stage (Phase 1/2) trials. 

Since vaccine development—from Phase 1 to full regulatory approval and widespread recognition—typically takes 7–15 years, it is virtually impossible to see a complete and licensed GAS vaccine emerge by 2026, 2027, or even 2029. Consequently, I forecast the bare minimum probability for all resolution dates up to 2029. For the 5-year outlook (2031), there is a slim window for success if an ongoing candidate receives fast-track designation and experiences no clinical setbacks, but base rates for such rapid success remain low. Thus, probability bumps up marginally. For the 10-year horizon (2036), the probability rises noticeably closer to 0.5 (I placed it at 0.28), acknowledging increasing funding (e.g., from CARB-X, Open Philanthropy) and improved technological platforms (like mRNA and novel adjuvants) that are actively pushing GAS candidates toward later-stage trials over the coming decade.

Ju Wenjun's rating as of early April 2026 is 2559. To reach a 1% higher rating, she must achieve approximately 2585 Elo (+26 points). Though she has historically reached over 2600 and was around 2580 in April 2025, she has since slid to 2559 at age 35. For veteran chess players, gaining 26 rating points requires an exceptional, sustained performance that is biologically and statistically improbable (base rate for such a gain is very low). In the short term (May 2026), this is effectively impossible as no such performance is ongoing. Over a longer horizon, her rating is more likely to decline or stagnate as she ages, or she may become inactive altogether, stalling her rating. I provide a baseline 0.05 probability for the near-term given the extreme difficulty of a +26 point jump. Per instructions, my uncertainty scales upward substantially for the longer time horizons (extending out to 10 years), making the estimates for distant dates approach 0.5, accounting for the unlikeliness of reaching the peak again vs. possible global rating inflation or structural changes in FIDE ratings over a decade.

Ju Wenjun's rating as of early April 2026 is 2559. To reach a 1% higher rating, she must achieve approximately 2585 Elo (+26 points). Though she has historically reached over 2600 and was around 2580 in April 2025, she has since slid to 2559 at age 35. For veteran chess players, gaining 26 rating points requires an exceptional, sustained performance that is biologically and statistically improbable (base rate for such a gain is very low). In the short term (May 2026), this is effectively impossible as no such performance is ongoing. Over a longer horizon, her rating is more likely to decline or stagnate as she ages, or she may become inactive altogether, stalling her rating. Wikipedia currently lists 'No' for a Toxoplasmosis vaccine. A review of recent literature and clinical trial databases (as of April 2026) reveals that all toxoplasmosis vaccine candidates are in the pre-clinical or veterinary stages (mostly being tested in mice and cats). There are no human clinical trials underway. The typical timeline for vaccine development from preclinical stage to approval is over 10 years, and parasite vaccines are notoriously difficult to develop, requiring long trials. Therefore, the chance of a vaccine being approved and added to this Wikipedia page in the next few years is near zero. Even by 2036, it is highly unlikely that a vaccine would complete Phase I-III trials and gain approval, though the probability is slightly higher. The early year estimates are floored at 0.05 to account for the possibility of short-lived Wikipedia vandalism on the resolution date.

The resolution criteria requires Hikaru Nakamura to increase his FIDE Elo rating by 1% compared to his rating on 2026-04-26. 

Based on the Wikipedia page "FIDE rankings" as of April 2026, his rating is exactly 2810. As FIDE updates ratings monthly on the 1st, his baseline on 2026-04-26 will definitively be 2810. A 1% increase requires him to gain 28.1 points to reach 2838.1.

An Elo of 2839 has only ever been achieved by four players in chess history (Carlsen, Kasparov, Caruana, and Aronian). Nakamura's all-time peak is 2816, achieved in 2015. At 38 years old in 2026, it is mathematically and historically almost impossible for him to achieve a +28 point organic gain against 2700-2800 opposition. Recent evidence from the 2026 FIDE Candidates also shows Nakamura drawing and losing to players like Caruana and Sindarov, making short-term rating spikes to the required level entirely unfeasible. Furthermore, FIDE recently amended regulations (eliminating the 400-point difference rule) to prevent high-ranked players from farming points from much weaker opposition, which cuts off an alternative route to 2839.

For the nearest resolution dates (up to mid-2026), the probability is practically 0, set here to the minimum valid probability of 0.02 to respect system limits. Over broader time horizons (2031, 2036), the slightly increased probabilities (up to 0.15) encompass systemic uncertainties, primarily the slight possibility that FIDE deliberately enacts a massive general Elo inflation across top-level chess down the line. However, barring fundamental rating scale recalibrations, reaching 2839 intrinsically remains a near-impossible feat.

There is currently no human vaccine for giardiasis, and recent searches show no ongoing human clinical trials. Scientific papers from 2021, 2022, and 2025 all confirm that while preclinical research is happening, no human vaccine has yet been licensed or reached advanced clinical stages. The only existing vaccine is an animal one (GiardiaVax), which has existed since 1999 but has not been included on this Wikipedia page, reflecting its focus on human applications. Given the typical 10+ year timeline for vaccine development, the probability of a human vaccine being developed and listed before 2029 is virtually zero. However, my predictions use the minimum allowed probability limit of 0.05 for the near-term dates and then scale up slightly for the 3-year, 5-year, and 10-year horizons to account for the possibility of a preclinical candidate advancing through trials or potential Wikipedia redefinitions.

Alireza Firouzja's current Wikipedia FIDE rating as of 2026-04-26 is exactly 2759. To reach a rating at least 1% higher, he would need a rating of 2787 (2759 * 1.01 = 2786.59). Firouzja failed to qualify for the 2026 Candidates and has been largely inactive in classical chess since September 2025, which means his May 2026 rating list will almost certainly remain at 2759. FIDE updates lists on the 1st of each month; therefore, the first two resolution dates (May 3 and May 26) will use the May 1 list, making resolution effectively zero (P=0.02 to account for error bounds). For July 25, he will have played Norway Chess, but gaining +28 Elo from a single top-level classical tournament requires an almost impossible performance (+7 or +8 score). By October 2026 and 2027, he may play more events (like Sinquefield Cup) giving him a slightly better chance of regaining form, though it remains a steep climb. Long-term (2029-2036), Firouzja's prodigious talent and previous peak of 2804 suggest he absolutely has the ceiling to hit 2787 again. However, probabilities are moderated by his well-documented struggles with chess motivation, dual focus on fashion, and the FIDE rule that inactive players are removed from the Top 100 list. If he retires or drops below 2787, it will resolve NO. Thus, while distant dates see an increase in probability, they are heavily constrained by inactivity risks and rating deflation trends at the very top.

Men's swimming 400m freestyle WR holders tend to retain the title of WR holder for extended periods (e.g. Kieren Perkins 7 years, Ian Thorpe 10 years, Paul Biedermann 16 years - though Biedermann's was aided by a super-suit). Lukas Märtens recently broke the 16-year-old record in April 2025, posting a 3:39.96 as a 23-year-old. He holds a ~0.7s margin over strong active competitors like Sam Short and Ahmed Hafnaoui, but those competitors are also quite young and improving. The probability of Märtens retaining the status of WR holder over short timeframes (e.g., 2026) is extremely high, only moderately challenged by smaller championships. As we project into the late 2020s and beyond, the likelihood of a competitor surpassing his times increases significantly, lowering the probabilities progressively down to 0.15 for 2036 (reflecting over a decade into the future where new talent typically disrupts current records).

Kawasaki disease is an inflammatory condition whose precise etiology remains unknown, though it is widely suspected to be a post-infectious immunologic response to one or potentially multiple unidentified agents (similar to MIS-C). Because there is no specific known infectious agent, there are currently no vaccines in development for Kawasaki disease itself. Web searches confirm this, as current research intersecting "vaccines" and "Kawasaki disease" focuses almost entirely on evaluating KD as an adverse effect of other vaccines or establishing immunization safety protocols for children recovering from KD. 

Given that there are no vaccine candidates in preclinical or clinical stages, and that vaccine development typically takes 5–15 years even after a pathogen is clearly identified, the probability of a vaccine being successfully developed and listed on Wikipedia in the short to medium term (2026–2029) is at the system's floor limit. By the 2031 and 2036 horizons, there is a small structural chance of a medical breakthrough identifying a single viral trigger and culminating in a viable vaccine, raising the probabilities slightly to reflect increasing uncertainty over longer timeframes.

Nepomniachtchi is currently ranked 18th in the FIDE April 2026 list. While he is very close to drops to 19th or 20th, he is a historically strong Top-5 player who will likely experience regression to the mean over the next year and regain a higher rank (i.e. better than 18). However, due to ambiguity in whether "as high or higher" resolves mathematically (>=18) or categorically (ranks #1-#18), and expected aging effects for distant dates (by 2036 he will be 46), uncertainty must be maximized for the longest horizons. The very near-term dates rely heavily on the base rate (0.68) and the structural lag of Wikipedia updates (May 3rd might still reflect his exact April rank). I have steadily increased uncertainty toward 0.5 for distant dates as instructed.

Relapsing fever is caused by Borrelia species (e.g. B. recurrentis, B. hermsii, B. miyamotoi) that are distinct from Lyme disease-causing Borrelia. While there is a Lyme disease vaccine (VLA15) in Phase 3 clinical trials, there are no vaccines for Relapsing fever in clinical trials or nearing approval. Furthermore, creating a vaccine for Relapsing fever is biologically challenging due to the pathogen's antigenic variation strategies (variable major proteins). The disease also currently lacks the economic prioritization of major pharmaceutical pipelines since it is relatively rare and easily treated with cheap antibiotics (tetracycline/doxycycline). Given typical vaccine development timelines (5-10+ years from phase 1 to approval), it is highly improbable that a vaccine will be developed and added to the Wikipedia list within the next 5-10 years. Uncertainty increases slightly for the 2036 horizon due to possible longer-term advancements or a shift in focus if tick-borne illnesses surge, but base rates remain extremely low.

The question asks if Anish Giri will have an Elo rating on several future dates that is at least 1% higher than his rating on 2026-04-26. A 1% increase over a top-level Elo (around 2753 to 2765) is approx +27.5 points. If he gained 12 points in the Candidates to end up around 2765, you need to add 27.6, meaning a target around 2792.6. If the baseline is 2753, he needs 2780.5. Short term (days to months), gaining 28 points without playing 2-3 tournaments and scoring massively in them is nearly impossible (he would need to win the equivalent of another candidates with +4 or better). Therefore, short-term dates (2026-05 to 2026-10) have exceedingly low probabilities (~1-2%). For the medium term (2027), there's a slight increase to 3%. Long term (2029-2036), Giri, born in 1994, will be in his mid-30s to early 40s. While 30s is often a peak or plateau period, achieving such a significant rating jump (to ~2780-2795) is difficult, yet historical precedence (his peak being ~2798) makes it non-zero over a span of many years. I'll increase uncertainty/probability over time as requested, ending up at 8% for 2036. The FIDE Elo +1% empirical base rate is 0.007, acting as a strong anchor, but we adjust up slightly over the long horizons due to age/peak possibilities and uncertainty.

Metagonimiasis is caused by a small intestinal fluke (Metagonimus yokogawai) and is highly treatable with cheap and effective medications like praziquantel. It is not a major global health priority, and there are currently no serious vaccine candidates even in early clinical trials for humans, let alone near approval. Because developing a vaccine from scratch (even with modern mRNA technology) for a eukaryotic parasite typically takes over a decade and huge funding (which is completely absent here), the true chance of a vaccine being successfully developed, approved, and listed on Wikipedia is practically zero. The probabilities are kept at the minimum bound (0.02) for the near-term dates and raised very slightly for the 2030s to account for general uncertainty, paradigm shifts in vaccinology, or potential temporary Wikipedia vandalism on the date of resolution.

Because developing a vaccine from scratch (even with modern mRNA technology) for a eukaryotic parasite typically takes over a decade and huge funding (which is completely absent here), the true chance of a vaccine being successfully developed, approved, and listed on Wikipedia is practically zero. The probabilities are kept at the minimum bound (0.02) for the near-term dates and raised very slightly for the 2030s to account for general uncertainty, paradigm shifts in vaccinology, or potential temporary Wikipedia vandalism on the date of resolution.

Vincent Keymer was ranked #5 on the April 2026 FIDE list with a rating of 2762. In April 2026, a major classical event (the FIDE Candidates Tournament) took place, featuring rising star Javokhir Sindarov (April rating 2745). Sindarov won the Candidates with an incredible undefeated 10.0/14 score. Such a performance against elite opposition will easily net him ~25-35 rating points, propelling him well past Keymer's 2762 into the top 5 on the upcoming May 2026 FIDE rating list. Since Keymer played in the Grenke Freestyle Chess Open (a Fischer random variant, untouched by classical FIDE rating rules) in April, his classical rating will likely remain unchanged, meaning he will be pushed to at least #6 on the May 2026 list. Thus, the probability of him retaining a Top 5 spot in early May is extremely low, limited primarily by the risk of Wikipedia taking a few days to update the table for the May 3rd resolution. Long-term, Keymer is a brilliant prodigy alongside other young talents (Gukesh, Pragg, Sindarov, Abdusattorov), and his probability of returning to the Top 5 rises steadily over the next few years.

The target rating is ~2527 (1% higher than 2502). Attaining a 25-point increase at the 2500+ classical level in mere weeks or months is highly unlikely absent a monumental, low-base-rate streak in a major event. Thus, probabilities for May, July, and October 2026 are kept extremely low (2% to 8%). However, Polina Shuvalova is young (born 2001, making her 25 in 2026), active (qualified for the Women's Grand Prix 2026-2027), and possesses high tactical strength (recently hitting 2939 blitz on Chesscom). Over 1 to 10 years, the probability of her crossing the 2527 mark (roughly top 8 women globally) rises moderately. Because uncertainty and the potential for long-term improvement (or FIDE rating inflation) increase over time, probabilities scale progressively toward 0.5 for the 2029, 2031, and 2036 horizons.

R Praggnanandhaa's FIDE rating on 2026-04-01 was 2741, meaning a 1% increase requires him to reach an Elo of at least 2768.41 (effectively 2769). Recent news as of April 2026 indicates he is having a poor performance at the 2026 FIDE Candidates tournament (4.5 points in 11 rounds), meaning his live rating is actively dropping below 2741. Thus, it is mathematically impossible for him to gain enough points to achieve a 2769 rating by the May 2026 FIDE rating lists, making the May resolution dates highly improbable (0.02). Over the next 3 to 6 months (July and October 2026), a jump of 35-40 Elo points is still extremely difficult, though slightly more possible if he goes on a streak. Looking further out, Praggnanandhaa previously reached a career high live rating of ~2778 in mid-2025, which proves he has the potential to clear the 2769 threshold. As a 20-year-old rising star, his trajectory remains very promising in the medium term, pushing probabilities for 2027 and 2029 up to 0.30 and 0.45 respectively. For the most distant dates (2031, 2036), his Elo could realistically stabilize above or below 2769 depending on whether he maintains top-10 status throughout his career; corresponding to standard forecasting practice, these probabilities are set around 0.50 reflecting maximum uncertainty.

There is currently no approved vaccine for Tinea pedis (athlete's foot), nor are there any approved human vaccines against any fungal pathogens. Tinea pedis is a mild, surface-level infection that is overwhelmingly managed with extremely cheap, effective over-the-counter topical antifungals. Thus, there is extremely little therapeutic incentive or funding backing the development of a vaccine. While a 2024 scientific paper presented a computational "bioinformatics" design for an mRNA vaccine targeting Trichophyton rubrum, this represents in silico hypothesis generation rather than tangible clinical pipeline development. Transitioning from computational design to pre-clinical and then through Phase 1-3 human trials typically takes 10-15 years minimum, even for heavily incentivized targets. It is effectively impossible for a vaccine to be developed and listed on Wikipedia's formal list in the short-to-medium horizons, and still highly unlikely even by the final 2036 resolution date. Accordingly, probabilities are kept at the mandated 0.05 minimum for near dates with only minor drift towards 0.5 over the multi-year horizons to reflect strict temporal uncertainty.
